definition: A five-signal early warning framework for EM FX crises validated across 15+ episodes with 80% hit rate at 4-5 signals and 45% hit rate at 3 signals. Pre-crisis sequencing is consistent: reserves depletion → forward stress → capital controls → devaluation. Currently 8-12 economies show 3+ signals. Vulnerability has rotated from current-account-driven crises to fiscal-monetary conflict.
evidence: 80% hit rate at 4-5 signals across 15+ episodes
Consistent pre-crisis sequencing documented by generalist_02
CA crises are self-correcting while fiscal-monetary crises create vicious circles
Recent crises (Turkey 2021-23, Sri Lanka 2022, Pakistan 2022-23, Egypt 2023-24) refute Goodhart's Law degradation
open_questions: Which of the current 8-12 economies at 3+ signals are most vulnerable?
Does the rotation to fiscal-monetary conflict change the sequencing timeline?
What is the false positive rate and has it changed over time?
